text.title: GitLab Duo Agent Platform Developer Flow Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the tracker-gitlab-duo-developer-flow methodology plus the `skip-this-methodology` safety rule. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: GitLab version MUST be ≥ 18.8. Earlier versions hit the locking bug + feature-flag config issues in incident #21171 and are out of scope.
rule.rationale: Running on pre-18.8 silently corrupts MR locking, producing duplicate merge attempts; the methodology cannot guarantee correctness there.

rule.statement: Flows MUST be triggered by issue labels (`agent:implement`, `agent:review`, `agent:ci-fix`) or scheduled pipelines. Raw API triggers from external services are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Labels and scheduled pipelines route through the same authorization model as humans; raw API triggers bypass it.

rule.statement: Flow output MUST still pass the project's approval-rule policy before merge. Flows MUST NOT use API tokens that bypass approval rules.
rule.rationale: Bypass tokens defeat the entire compliance regime; humans approve, agents propose.

rule.statement: Developer Flow → issue→MR. Code Review Flow → automated review. Software Development Flow → multi-step plan-before-execute. CI/CD Flows → pipeline migrations + failing-build fixes. Routing MUST match task type.
rule.rationale: Reusing one flow for every task defeats the purpose of named flows; routing must be deliberate.

rule.statement: Every flow invocation MUST emit a flow log entry (`duo_flow_invocations` audit event) including label trigger, actor, MR ID, and outcome.
rule.rationale: Compliance audits require a per-invocation log; without it, flows look like ghost automation.

rule.statement: Skip this methodology when the preconditions in `## Applies If` are not all true, or any item in `## Skip If` holds. The decision tree routes to this rule on those branches.
rule.rationale: A methodology that runs on the wrong input wastes tokens and produces misleading artifacts. The explicit skip rule is the safety valve.
